name: Aebleskivers
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat the yolks of the eggs with the buttermilk.
2. Mix together the sugar, salt, flour, baking powder, baking soda, and cardamom.
3. Beat the egg whites until stiff.
4. Mix the egg yolk-buttermilk mixture with the dry ingredients. Add the egg whites, carefully folding them in so that they don't break down.
5. Heat the aebleskive pan. Put 1 teaspoon of salad oil in each hole and fill completely with batter. Let bake until slightly crusty on bottom.
6. Turn the aebleskivers slightly with a knitting needle or skewer. Continue cooking, turning the ball to keep it from burning, until the knitting needle comes out clean when stuck in the center.
7. Serve aebelskivers hot with powdered sugar, jam, and jelly.
Notes included with recipe: For those of you who are unfamiliar with aebelskive, it is a baked delight made with a pancake-like batter, cooked in a special pan that turns the aebelskive into a crusty ball, and is known as the "Danish Doughnut." As you can tell, it is a little difficult to give an easy definition other than to tell you that it is good enough to go ahead and order the pans if you are likely to serve breakfasts and brunches to family and friends. Aebelskive pans are available through most gourmet cookware shops or can be ordered from Pea Soup Andersen's Santa Ynez Valley Wine Center, (805)-688-5581. Source: Pea Soup Andersen's Scandinavian-American Cookbook
